articles.title: ENG v IND 2026: ‘Both KL Rahul and Ishan Kishan can play if..’- Abhinav Mukund suggests India makes this change for Lord’s ODI
articles.description: In the upcoming third ODI against England at Lord's, former opener Abhinav Mukund believes that Team India may use both Ishan Kishan and KL Rahul in the starting lineup. Shivam Dube can be dropped because he isn't making much of an impact with the bat, he continued. On Sunday, July 19, India will play England in the third and last ODI of the three-match series at Lord's Cricket Ground. India won the first game in Birmingham by six wickets, while England won the match in Cardiff by four wickets, leaving the series tied at one-all. Rahul was unavailable for the second ODI due to illness, and hence Ishan Kishan got the chance to play. Mukund opined that Kishan can retain his place in the playing XI for the Lord's clash even if Rahul is fit and available. " You've got to decide then what happens with Shivam Dube. Are you primarily picking him as a bowler or a batter? If he is not giving you runs with the bat, then you can afford to play both KL Rahul and Ishan Kishan. For Shivam Dube, Kuldeep Yadav does come in. You play with seven batters, with Axar Patel batting at No. 7, and then Kuldeep comes in and gives you five proper bowling options ,” Mukund said on Cricinfo . Additionally, Mukund expressed his excitement about the potential of young pacer Gurnoor Brar. Mukund praised the right-arm fast bowler and said he might be a contender for the 2027 World Cup. " I would love to see more of Gurnoor Brar. He has been a terrific find for India starting from the Afghanistan series. He shows a lot of heart and bowls fast—145. He is a candidate for the 2027 World Cup. In the absence of Harshit Rana, Gurnoor Brar has stepped into his shoes very well. I'd love to see him pick up more wickets, ” he said. Brar has had a good start to his ODI career, taking 11 wickets with a best of 3/27.
articles.aiTag: ONLY AVAILABLE IN PROFESSIONAL AND CORPORATE PLANS

articles.title: ENG v IND 2026: “The guy is not retiring”- Aakash Chopra says Rohit Sharma hopefully scores a ton in Lord’s ODI
articles.description: Ahead of the third ODI against England, former Indian cricketer Aakash Chopra predicted that Rohit Sharma will not retire. On Sunday, July 19, Lord's will host the last One-Day International (ODI) in the three-match series between India and England. He did, however, hope that if it was the final game of the ex-India captain, he would score a century. Devajit Saikia, the secretary of the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI), denied rumors that it was Rohit's final international match. He pointed out that even if Rohit doesn't appear to be retiring, if this is his final game, he should score a hundred. " I really hope that India wins, and I really hope that Rohit Sharma scores the runs this time around. Firstly, he is not going anywhere. The guy is not retiring. However, if the conversation goes in the direction where it's finalized and you tell Rohit that you are no longer continuing with him, I hope it's not. Still, if it were to be the last game, I want him to score a century ," Chopra stated on his YouTube channel . " Whenever he says goodbye to this format, he should leave with a lasting impression. That's my only small wish. It's probably the beauty of cricket that, despite playing so much cricket, being an absolute legend of a cricketer, and having a phenomenal international career, you reach a stage where the relevance of the match increases. You making runs becomes necessary ," he added. Rohit Sharma had missed the 2011 World Cup and led India to the final of the 2023 World Cup. The 39-year-old has repeatedly expressed a desire to play the 2027 World Cup in South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Namibia. According to Aakash Chopra, there is no need to worry about Rohit Sharma playing until the 2027 ODI World Cup. " It's not an option. It's almost a mandate that you have to score runs now because the questions are becoming slightly louder. Are they necessary? I don't think so because we will play 40 ODIs in the next one-and-a-half years. What you have to do today, you can do that after 10 matches as well ," he said. Chopra emphasized that he is eager to observe the opener's strategy by adding that the 39-year-old will need to score runs due to the mood that has been created. " However, an atmosphere like that has been created where you feel this is an important one. So, Rohit Sharma should score runs. He was extremely scratchy in the last match. He became slightly defensive as well in the first 10 overs. I will be very curious to see how he plays ," Chopra concluded. Sharma has not been in the best of form in ODIs in 2026, with only one fifty in the format. In the ongoing ODI series, Rohit has scored 11 runs off 21 balls in the first game and then took 47 balls to score 26 runs in the second ODI.
articles.aiTag: ONLY AVAILABLE IN PROFESSIONAL AND CORPORATE PLANS

articles.title: Will IND vs ENG 3rd ODI 2026 at Lord's Be Rohit Sharma's Last One-Day International for India?
articles.description: As India faces England in the decisive 3rd ODI at Lord's today, July 19, 2026, intense speculation surrounds Rohit Sharma's ODI future. Despite his childhood coach's view that he aims for the 2027 World Cup, reports suggest the BCCI selectors have informed the 39-year-old he is not in their plans beyond this series.

articles.title: Hope wants West Indies wins in Sir Garry’s name
articles.description: BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (CMC):West Indies captain Shai Hope is hoping that the death of the legendary Sir Garfield Sobers inspires his troops to victory in their One-Day International series against New Zealand.Sir Garry, one of Barbados’ National Heroes and widely regarded as the best cricketer the world has ever seen, passed away on Friday at the age of 89.In 93 Test matches for the West Indies from 1954 to 1974, Sir Garry scored 8,032 runs at an average of 57.78 and also took 235 wickets.
